title: Health-Related Quality of Life for Long-Acting Octreotide versus Placebo in Patients with Metastatic Midgut Neuroendocrine Tumors in the Phase 3 PROMID Trial.
abstract: In the phase IIIb PROMID study, octreotide long-acting significantly extended time to tumor progression compared with placebo in treatment-naïve patients with well-differentiated metastatic midgut neuroendocrine tumors. We report post hoc analyses for health-related quality of life (HRQoL).
